title: Hearing concern in child

investigations.text: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.
investigations.rationale: Supports hearing difficulty, communication, behaviour, otoscopy, hearing testing and tympanometry for child hearing concern; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports concerns and functional impact, hearing difficulty, speech or behaviour context, balance, URTI and AOM history, mouth breathing, adenoid context, otoscopy, developmental assessment, hearing testing, and tympanometry.

treatment_and_management.text: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

treatment_and_management.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged reassessment, interval hearing impact, changing needs, and documented questions without generating an interval or intervention.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, onset, side, fluctuation, speech, school impact, infections, family history, otoscopy, hearing tests, diagnosis and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for child hearing concern.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
